_meta.what: RABIE provisional pass over all sector lenses (occasion_facts.json sector_lenses)
_meta.law: RABIE_MIND taste law — KILL: generic/any-brand, non-Saudi, offer-energy on emotional occasions, Western-aspirational, abstract. REWARD: concrete Saudi-lived WHO+WHEN scene.
_meta.rubric: 0-2 = flagged for regen (<3). 3 = good default. 4 = earned with concrete Saudi anchors. 5 = reserved (Mohamed's eye).
_meta.built_from: scripts/build_sector_lenses.py output read in full this shift

saudi_founding_day.why: Strongest set. Concrete heritage anchors: Dir'iyah, Najdi dishes, sadu/calligraphy, mud-brick architecture, palm/oud scents, 18th-century Najdi clothing. Saudi-native, you can SEE it.

hajj_season.why: Deeply Saudi-native + WHO+WHEN stamped: Ihram tailors, qurban exchange, Makkah/Madinah prayer beads, heat-exhaustion clinics, hotel capacity. Real occasion lived through each sector.

eid_al_adha.why: Strong f&b/beauty (qurban meat to neighbors, henna night-before, WHO+WHEN). Tail weakens: real_estate 'prospective buyers explore properties' + healthcare 'community health workers' drift generic.

arab_mothers_day.why: Warm, family-centered, concrete in f&b/beauty/fashion. But healthcare (yoga subscription, wellness retreat) + real_estate (garden makeover, decor voucher) are Western-aspirational and forced.

ramadan.why: Concrete iftar/suhoor/taraweeh, grandparents sharing past-Ramadan stories (warm). real_estate 'open houses on Ramadan evenings' is a forced sell; retail mildly commercial.

riyadh_season.why: Themed zones, food trucks, concrete. Pulled down by repetitive 'themed zone' phrasing + commercial merch lean (temporary tattoos, limited-edition drinks).

mdl_beast.why: Festival concrete (glitter/face-paint before main acts, energy drinks midday, artist merch). 'rented villas for pre-festival parties' + 'rooftop views' drift, but acceptable for a youth festival.

esports_world_cup.why: Gaming cafes + late-night shawarma runs are a genuine Saudi touch. Weak tail: beauty (eye masks/nail art) + real_estate (gaming rooms/VR tours) are generic-aspirational.

weekly_offer.why: Offer-occasion done right: Saudi weekend rhythm (Thursday-night shawarma kickoff, Tuesday group lunches), concrete WHO+WHEN+day. Clarity over poetry — fits.

new_branch.why: Concrete opening-day/first-week structure, neighborhood residents on lunch breaks. Slightly templated across sectors ('opening day', 'after work') but lived and usable.

white_friday.why: Borderline. Saved by the correct MENA localization ('White Friday' not 'Black Friday'). Content itself leans Western-retail-generic (midnight alarms, unboxing videos, lining up outside stores).

eid_al_fitr.why: KILL — offer/discount energy injected into an emotional occasion: 'marketed as Eid beauty collections', 'promotional bundles', 'Eid promotions', 'pre-Eid sales', 'purchased during pre-Eid sales'. Eid is emotional, not a sale.

saudi_national_day.why: Moat-occasion reading any-country. Some grounding (Jareesh/Mandi, green/white, Saudi coffee) but half the sectors are generic-corporate/commercial: 'scavenger hunts by retail brands', 'morning yoga in public parks', 'evening meditation classes', 'networking events for industry leaders', 'real estate seminars'.

jeddah_season.why: CULTURAL RISK. Good local anchors (Balad, Corniche, Red Sea, King Abdulaziz Park) but 'romantic dinners for couples', 'beachwear', 'waterfront parties', 'beach-ready hairstyles' clash with Saudi modesty norms — a client could reject on culture grounds.

leap_conference.why: Globally generic, any-country tech conference, zero Saudi flavor + abstract: 'mocktails at evening mixers', 'VIP lounges', 'smart clothing', 'AI-driven wellness apps', 'proptech innovations'.

daily_post.why: Western-generic, not Saudi-lived: 'open house', 'browsing property listings late at night', 'lipstick before a 10am meeting', 'meditating before bedtime'. Any-brand-anywhere.

new_product.why: Any-brand-anywhere, no Saudi texture: 'trendy café', 'beauty influencer unboxing live on Instagram', 'fashionista at a launch event', 'envisioning their future'. Describes a stock launch, not Saudi life.

11_11_shopping.why: Generic global e-commerce, no Saudi flavor: 'unboxing on social media', 'browsing apps on the commute', 'TikTok hauls', 'late-night impulse buys driven by flash sales'.
